Class PlayerTest

java.lang.Object
models.PlayerTest
public class PlayerTest extends Object
Test class for the Player class. This class tests the creation and behavior of Player objects. It verifies if players are correctly created from scratch and from saved data, if invalid names throw exceptions, and if resources, score and Action Points are managed correctly.
Author:
Fabio Cruz, Tiago Silva

  • Constructor Details

    • PlayerTest

      public PlayerTest()

  • Method Details

    • setUp

      @BeforeEach void setUp()
      Creates a new player before each test. This ensures that every test starts with a fresh Player object.

    • successfullyCreatePlayerFromScratch

      @Test void successfullyCreatePlayerFromScratch()
      Tests if a player is successfully created using the basic constructor. The test verifies if the player is not null, if the name is correct, if the initial score is correct, and if all starting resources have the expected initial values.

    • successfullyCreatePlayerFromSave

      @Test void successfullyCreatePlayerFromSave()
      Tests if a player is successfully created using saved data. This test uses the save constructor to create a player with custom values for name, score, wood, stone, food and Action Points. It then verifies if all values were restored correctly.

    • playerBasicConstructorThrowsIllegalArgumentException

      @Test void playerBasicConstructorThrowsIllegalArgumentException()
      Tests if the basic constructor throws IllegalArgumentException when the player name is invalid. An empty name should not be accepted.

    • playerSaveConstructorThrowsIllegalArgumentException

      @Test void playerSaveConstructorThrowsIllegalArgumentException()
      Tests if the save constructor throws IllegalArgumentException when the player name is invalid. An empty name should not be accepted, even when loading a player from saved values.

    • successfullyResetTheAP

      @Test void successfullyResetTheAP()
      Tests if the player's Action Points are reset correctly. The player receives extra Action Points and then resetAP is called. The current Action Points should return to the player's base Action Points.

    • addActionPointsCorrectly

      @Test void addActionPointsCorrectly()
      Tests if base Action Points are added correctly. The test verifies if both the current Action Points and the base Action Points increase by the expected value.

    • addScoreCorrectly

      @Test void addScoreCorrectly()
      Tests if score is added correctly to the player. The test stores the score before adding points and then verifies if the score increased by the expected amount.

    • addResourcesCorrectly

      @Test void addResourcesCorrectly()
      Tests if resources are added correctly to the player's inventory. The test adds wood to the player and verifies if the quantity increased by the expected value.

    • removeHavingEnoughResources

      @Test void removeHavingEnoughResources()
      Tests if resources are removed correctly when the player has enough. The test removes wood from the player and verifies if the operation returns true and if the quantity was reduced correctly.

    • removeDontHavingEnoughResources

      @Test void removeDontHavingEnoughResources()
      Tests if removing resources fails when the player does not have enough. The inventory is cleared before trying to remove wood. The operation should return false.

    • cleaningInventoryCorrectly

      @Test void cleaningInventoryCorrectly()
      Tests if the inventory is cleared correctly. After calling clearInventory, all main resources and Action Points should be equal to zero.
